eraMap.title: The X3 30i THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: Silky smooth naturally aspirated straight-six — legendary BMW engine but oil leaks, VANOS and cooling system need attention
Early N20 turbo four — timing chain tensioner is the defining weak point; pre-2015 cars are highest risk
Revised N20 — timing chain guides improved but risk not eliminated
Modern B48 four-cylinder — 248 bhp, 350 Nm, significantly improved reliability but coolant leaks are a known issue
Latest B48 with mild-hybrid — 245 bhp, 350 Nm; proven B48 engine with added 48V MHEV complexity

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current X3 30i range. Older generations: see the Era Map above for E83/N52, F25/N20 and G01/B48-specific pricing. An N52B30 replacement motor costs on average £2,013 in good condition, with new or reconditioned engines up to £3,925.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an X3 30i engine typically takes 8–14 hours at £75–£110/hr (independent) or £130–£180/hr (London/SE dealer). N20 timing chain work is a significant job. [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

problems.title: N20 Timing Chain & Guide Failure (F25 pre-2015)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start lasting 1–3 seconds, whining noise while revving, engine warning light, loss of power, metallic noises from the front of the engine.
problems.typicalMileage: 80,000–120,000 km (50,000–75,000 miles) — symptoms can appear as early as 60,000 km
problems.replacementCost: £3,500–£6,500 (if chain snaps and valves meet pistons)【PLACEHOLDER】

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The N20's timing chain tensioner is the defining weak point of this engine generation. The plastic guides wear out, allowing the chain to slip and potentially causing catastrophic engine damage. Since the N20 is an interference engine, when the chain fails it's "basically fatal for the engine". Pre-2015 cars are the highest risk, with BMW extending the warranty on N20 timing chains to 7 years/70,000 miles. A cold-start rattle is the early warning sign — ignore it and you risk piston-valve contact and a full engine replacement. Regular oil changes with the correct BMW-approved oil are the best prevention. At the first sign of rattle, get it inspected immediately.

problems.title: B48 Cooling System Leaks (G01 — Water Pump, Thermostat, Oil Filter Housing)
problems.symptoms: Low coolant warning, coolant loss with no visible external leak, sweet smell from engine bay, temperature fluctuations, overheating.
problems.repairCost: £400–£1,500 (water pump, thermostat, coolant hose, or oil filter housing replacement)【PLACEHOLDER】
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (unless overheating leads to engine damage)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant loss is detected

problems.recommendation: The B48 engine has a well-documented cooling system weakness. The cooling systems are "proving troublesome with various leaks". The plastic oil filter housing can crack. Coolant hoses and plastic coolant flanges can become brittle due to heat cycles, leading to cracks and leaks. One owner reported a coolant connector that "just crumbled in my finger tips". If you notice coolant loss or temperature fluctuations, have the system pressure-tested immediately. Proactive replacement of known weak coolant hoses and the oil filter housing at 60,000 miles is recommended.

problems.title: N52 / N20 Oil Leaks (Valve Cover, Oil Filter Housing, Oil Pan)
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the vehicle, burning oil smell, visible oil seepage around the valve cover, oil filter housing or oil pan, low oil level warning.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; oil leaks can damage other components

problems.recommendation: Oil leaks are among the most common issues across all X3 30i generations. The N52 and N20 engines are "especially prone to gasket wear". The oil filter housing gasket on N20 and N52 engines fails regularly and causes oil to drip onto exhaust components, which produces a burning smell and requires immediate attention. Valve cover gasket leaks are very common once the engine reaches 80,000–120,000 miles. If you notice oil spots under the car or a burning smell, have it inspected promptly.

problems.title: N52 Electric Water Pump & Cooling System Failure (E83)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss, overheating, temperature fluctuations, sweet smell from engine bay, low coolant warning.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (unless overheating leads to engine damage)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant loss or overheating occurs

problems.recommendation: The N52's electric water pump is a known weak point, typically failing around 80,000–120,000 miles. The pump can fail without warning, leading to sudden overheating. If you notice temperature fluctuations or coolant loss, have the cooling system inspected immediately. Proactive replacement of the water pump and thermostat at 100,000-mile intervals is recommended preventative maintenance.

problems.title: N52 VANOS Solenoid Failure (E83)
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, hesitation on acceleration, loss of power at low RPM, reduced fuel economy, check engine light.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon; can affect variable valve timing

problems.recommendation: The N52's VANOS solenoids can fail, causing rough running and loss of power. If you experience rough idle or hesitation, have the VANOS system inspected promptly. Replacing or cleaning the solenoids is a relatively straightforward repair.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW X3 30i — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Catch it early — far cheaper than engine replacement
Diagnosis and repair is straightforward; catch it before overheating
Piston-valve contact means little is left to repair

buyingChecks.title: Buying This X3 30i? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with oil changes at 8,000–10,000 mile intervals
* E83 (2006–2010): N52 with no oil leaks and cooling system in good order
* F25 (2010–2017): timing chain tensioner replaced or inspected (post-2015 cars are lower risk)
* G01 (2017+): no coolant loss and cooling system in good order
* No cold-start rattle on any generation
avoidIf: * No proof of regular oil changes — these engines are unforgiving of neglect
* F25 with cold-start rattle — timing chain work is imminent and expensive
* Any coolant loss with no diagnosis — B48 cooling system repairs can be complex
* Any engine warning light without diagnosis — these engines can hide expensive faults
* "Cheap" examples — the purchase price may be the least of your worries

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The BMW X3 30i has evolved significantly over three generations. The E83's N52 straight-six is a silky-smooth naturally aspirated legend — reliable but with well-documented oil leaks and cooling system concerns. The F25's N20 turbo four-cylinder offers strong performance but the timing chain tensioner is a critical weak point, especially on pre-2015 cars. The G01's B48 four-cylinder is a significant step forward in reliability, with the timing chain moved to the front of the engine — but coolant leaks are the new watchpoint. Across all generations, one rule applies: oil-change discipline is everything. BMW's extended service intervals are widely considered inadequate — treat your X3 30i to 8,000–10,000 mile oil changes and it will reward you with hundreds of thousands of miles of driving pleasure.

cta.label: → Compare X3 30i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X3 30i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattle lasting more than 1–2 seconds (N20 timing chain warning)
* Service History — Including which engine era (N52/N20/B48) and confirmed oil-change discipline (8k–10k miles max)
* Oil Leaks — Check for oil around the valve cover, oil filter housing and oil pan
* Coolant Level — Check cold and hot; any loss indicates potential cooling system issues
* Engine Temperature — Any fluctuations? Water pump may be failing (N52) or coolant leak (B48)
* Idle Quality — Any roughness? VANOS issues (N52) or other concerns
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Timing, cooling, or VANOS codes are most common
* MOT History — Check for emissions failures (often related to fuel system or PCV issues)

items.label: SECTION 8 — FAQ
items.value: 1. How much does a BMW X3 30i engine replacement cost in the UK?

items.label: A reconditioned X3 30i engine typically costs £2,800–£8,500 supply only depending on generation. An N52B30 replacement motor costs on average £2,013 in good condition, with new or reconditioned engines up to £3,925. Fitting adds £800–£1,500 at independent specialist rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.
items.value: 2. Is the BMW X3 30i engine reliable?

items.label: It depends on which generation you're asking about. The N52 (E83, 2006–2010) is a legendary naturally aspirated straight-six — smooth and reliable but with well-documented oil leaks and cooling system concerns. The N20 (F25, 2010–2017) has a well-documented timing chain tensioner issue — pre-2015 cars are the highest risk. The B48 (G01, 2017+) is significantly more reliable, with the timing chain moved to the front, but coolant leaks are a known issue. Across all, oil-change discipline is everything.
items.value: 3. What are the common problems with the BMW X3 30i?

items.label: N20 timing chain wear (the defining weak point of the F25 generation), B48 cooling system leaks (G01 — water pump, thermostat, oil filter housing), N52/N20 oil leaks (valve cover, oil filter housing, oil pan), N52 electric water pump failure, and N52 VANOS solenoid failure. See the Era Map above for generation-specific advice.
items.value: 4. Is it worth replacing a BMW X3 30i engine, or should I scrap the car?

items.label: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept E83 X3 (values £3,000–£8,000), a £3,000–£4,000 recon is 30–70% of the car's value — borderline. Against a £8,000–£15,000 F25, a £4,000–£5,000 recon is 30–50% — often worth it. Against a £15,000–£30,000 G01, a £4,500–£5,500 recon is 15–35% — worth it. The BMW scrap threshold of 70% means evaluating the whole vehicle and its condition before committing.
items.value: 5. How do I find my BMW X3 30i engine code?

items.label: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: E83 (2006–2010) = N52B30; F25 (2010–2017) = N20B20; G01 (2017–present) = B48B20 (B46 in US market). Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.
items.value: 6. Can I repair the N20 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

items.label: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain, tensioner and guide replacement costs £800–£2,000. BMW extended the warranty on N20 timing chains to 7 years/70,000 miles. However, if the chain snaps, a full engine replacement (£3,500–£6,500) is usually required. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.
items.value: Disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data from our network of engine specialists. Actual costs vary by variant, engine code, damage severity and workshop location. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes. Every engine supplied through EnginesMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited-mileage warranty — always confirm terms with your chosen supplier.
